world.title: Texas student shoots teacher in deadly high school incident
world.description: A 15-year-old student shot a teacher at a Texas high school and then fatally shot himself Monday, according to authorities, who were still investigating what led to the early morning attack.

world.title: Paper Finds That Leading AI Chatbots Like ChatGPT and Claude Remain Incredibly Sycophantic, Resulting in Twisted Effects on Users
world.description: A study found that sycophancy is pervasive among chatbots, and that bots are more likely than human peers to affirm a person's bad behavior.

world.title: Earth’s ‘Energy Imbalance’ Hits New High as Emissions Keep Rising in 2025, WMO Reports
world.description: The Earth has reached a record “energy imbalance”, with the atmosphere taking on far more heat than it can release and ocean warming reaching new heights, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) warned last week, in a State of the Global Climate report that showed greenhouse gas emissions continuing to rise through 2025.

world.title: UN launches Global Mechanism on ICT security, elects chair for 2026-2027
world.description: The UN held the organisational session of its new Global Mechanism on ICT security, electing El Salvador’s Ambassador Egriselda López as chair for 2026–2027, while member states emphasised implementation of existing cybersecurity commitments and capacity development.

world.description: Deceased was injured in crash on Newry Road earlier this month

world.title: The oil shock has completely reshaped Australia's petrol price cycle
world.description: Petrol stations in our biggest cities hiked prices ahead of global oil prices, but experts say their profit margins are now much slimmer.

business.title: Global trial shows targeted heart drug reduces obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy in youth
business.description: A global clinical trial involving The Hospital for Sick Children (SickKids) has found that mavacamten—a heart medication previously approved for adults—can safely and effectively improve the flow of blood through compromised left ventricles in youth with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM). Published in the New England Journal of Medicine, the study is the first phase 3 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial conducted in youth with HCM, the most common inherited type of heart disease.

business.title: SEC goes ‘back to basics’ with capital markets focus - Willkie Farr & Gallagher partner Edward Best, with Troy Hooper - podcast
business.description: The SEC is somewhat unsurprisingly shifting “back to basics” under the Trump 2.0 administration, according to Edward Best, a partner at Willkie Farr & Gallagher.

science.title: Cornerstone model of evolutionary biology built on math flaw, study argues
science.description: New research is significantly revising a widely cited evolutionary model, the Inhibitory Cascade Mode (ICM). Benjamin Auerbach, professor in the Department of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, and Charles Roseman, associate professor in the Department of Ecology, Evolution, and Biology at the University of Illinois–Urbana-Champaign, published their findings in Evolution.

science.title: Physicists Find First Experimental Evidence of Elusive Critical Point in Supercooled Water
science.description: By probing supercooled water with ultrafast lasers before it crystallizes, physicists at Stockholm University observed telltale signs of a long-theorized transition between two liquid states, including surging heat capacity and critical fluctuations.

science.title: Pairs of atoms observed existing in two places at once for the first time
science.description: Quantum physicists at ANU have observed atoms entangled in motion. "It's really weird for us to think that this is how the universe works," says Dr. Sean Hodgman from the ANU Research School of Physics. "You can read about it in a textbook, but it's really weird to think that a particle can be in two places at once."

science.title: NT rock art thousands of years old sheds new light on the mysterious Tasmanian tiger
science.description: The rock art reveals the Thylacine’s deep significance to Aboriginal peoples.
